components.name: Sodium chloride (NaCl)
components.evidence: Medium A ... exact composition: 18 g L-1 NaCl, 0.6 g L-1 KCl, 1.0 g L-1 NaNO3, 5.0 g L-1 MgSO4*7H2O ... (0.3 M NaCl)

components.name: Potassium chloride (KCl)
components.evidence: 0.6 g L-1 KCl ... Compared to 300 mM NaCl and 8 mM KCl in standard A+ medium

components.name: Sodium nitrate (NaNO3)
components.concentration: 110 mM (photobioreactor supplement; base Medium A contains 1.0 g/L = 11.8 mM)
components.evidence: 930 mL of Media A supplemented with 110 mM NaNO3, 1.1 mM FeCl3, 5.2 mM KH2PO4, 100 ug mL-1 kanamycin, 1 mM IPTG, and 100 ng mL-1 anhydrous tetracycline

components.name: Monopotassium phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.concentration: 5.2 mM (photobioreactor supplement; base Medium A contains 50 mg/L = 0.37 mM)
components.evidence: 930 mL of Media A supplemented with 110 mM NaNO3, 1.1 mM FeCl3, 5.2 mM KH2PO4

components.name: Ferric chloride hexahydrate (FeCl3*6H2O)
components.concentration: 1.1 mM (photobioreactor supplement; base Medium A contains 3.89 mg/L = 0.014 mM)
components.evidence: supplemented with 110 mM NaNO3, 1.1 mM FeCl3, 5.2 mM KH2PO4

components.name: Carbon dioxide (CO2)
components.evidence: CO2: 5% in gas phase at 0.3 L min-1 flow rate; photoautotrophic under ~350 umol PAR m-2 s-1

components.name: Boric acid (H3BO3)
components.evidence: The P1 trace metal solution (1000x) contains: 34.26 g L-1 H3BO3 ... 1 mL L-1 P1 trace metal solution

components.name: Manganese(II) chloride tetrahydrate (MnCl2*4H2O)
components.evidence: P1 trace metal solution (1000x) contains: ... 4.32 g L-1 MnCl2*4H2O

components.name: Zinc chloride (ZnCl2)
components.evidence: P1 trace metal solution (1000x) contains: ... 0.315 g L-1 ZnCl2

components.name: Molybdenum trioxide (MoO3, 85%)
components.evidence: P1 trace metal solution (1000x) contains: ... 0.03 g L-1 MoO3 (85%)

components.name: Copper(II) sulfate pentahydrate (CuSO4*5H2O)
components.evidence: P1 trace metal solution (1000x) contains: ... 0.003 g L-1 CuSO4*5H2O

components.name: Cobalt(II) chloride hexahydrate (CoCl2*6H2O)
components.evidence: P1 trace metal solution (1000x) contains: ... 0.01215 g L-1 CoCl2*6H2O

components.name: Vitamin B12 (cyanocobalamin)
components.evidence: 1 mL L-1 P1 trace metal solution, and 4 ug L-1 vitamin B12 ... the GBF media was supplemented with trace metals and vitamin B12 at the concentrations found in Media A+

media.base_medium: Medium A+ (Synechococcus sp. PCC 7002 marine medium; Tris-buffered pH 8.2, ~0.3 M NaCl, P1 trace metals + vitamin B12). Base = Medium A of Stevens & Porter 1980; the "+" denotes NaNO3 addition. In this paper the photobioreactor received large NaNO3, FeCl3 and KH2PO4 additions on top of the base.

sources_chased.what: Base Medium A/A+ recipe cited as ref 24 (Stevens SE Jr, Patterson, Myers 1973 J Phycol) and the canonical Stevens & Porter 1980 / Ludwig & Bryant 2011 formulation
sources_chased.how: WebSearch + WebFetch (Ludwig & Bryant PMC3468840 confirms A+ = Medium A + 1 mg/mL NaNO3; full recipe from Stevens & Porter 1980)

sources_chased.what: P1 trace metal solution (1000x) constituent salts
sources_chased.how: WebSearch (published P1 metal composition for Medium A)

media.notes: Organism is Synechococcus sp. PCC 7002 (strain TK.032); photoautotrophic, aerobic (air + 5% CO2 sparge, photosynthetic O2), so oxygen corrected from 'facultative' to 'aerobic'. Standard Medium A+ has NO ammonium: the sole N sources are nitrate (Medium A base NaNO3 plus the paper's 110 mM NaNO3 supplement), so the current EX_nh4_e/'Ammonium' entry is spurious and should be dropped. The current mapping of 'CO2 (5% CO2 gas sparge)' to EX_cobalt2_e is a mis-map: CO2 is the carbon source (map to EX_co2_e) and cobalt is a separate genuine trace element from CoCl2 in the P1 mix. Base salt/trace concentrations are from the cited Medium A (Stevens & Porter 1980; Ludwig & Bryant 2011); paper-specific supplements (NaNO3, FeCl3, KH2PO4) override the low base levels for those three.
